title: Introduction to soilFlux
text: Note: code vignette requires TensorFlow Keras installed. See ?soilFlux installation instructions. outputs shown representative examples training run swrc_example dataset. soilFlux package implements physics-informed one-dimensional convolutional neural network (CNN1D-PINN) estimating complete soil water retention curve (SWRC) continuous function matric potential. architecture physics constraints adapted Norouzi et al. (2025).

title: Introduction to soilFlux
text: soil water retention curve describes relationship volumetric water content (θ\theta, m³/m³) matric potential (expressed pF = log₁₀(h [cm])). key input hydrological land-surface models.

title: Introduction to soilFlux
code: # Install development version from GitHub remotes::install_github("hugo-rodrigues/soilFlux")  # Required Python backend (once per machine) tensorflow::install_tensorflow()

title: Introduction to soilFlux
code: library(soilFlux)  # 1. Load and prepare data -------------------------------------------------- data("swrc_example")   # example dataset bundled with the package  df <- prepare_swrc_data(   swrc_example,   depth_col = "depth",   fix_bd    = TRUE,   fix_theta = TRUE )  # Train / validation / test split (by PEDON_ID) ids      <- unique(df$PEDON_ID) set.seed(42) tr_ids   <- sample(ids, floor(0.7 * length(ids))) val_ids  <- sample(setdiff(ids, tr_ids), floor(0.15 * length(ids))) te_ids   <- setdiff(ids, c(tr_ids, val_ids))  train_df <- df[df$PEDON_ID %in% tr_ids,  ] val_df   <- df[df$PEDON_ID %in% val_ids, ] test_df  <- df[df$PEDON_ID %in% te_ids,  ]  # 2. Fit model -------------------------------------------------------------- fit <- fit_swrc(   train_df   = train_df,   x_inputs   = c("clay", "silt", "bd_gcm3", "soc", "Depth_num"),   val_df     = val_df,   epochs     = 60,   batch_size = 256,   lambdas    = norouzi_lambdas("norouzi"),   verbose    = TRUE )  # 3. Evaluate --------------------------------------------------------------- m <- evaluate_swrc(fit, test_df) print(m)  # 4. Dense SWRC curves ------------------------------------------------------ dense <- predict_swrc_dense(fit, newdata = test_df, n_points = 500)  # 5. Plot ------------------------------------------------------------------- plot_swrc(dense, obs_points = test_df,           obs_col   = "theta_n",           facet_row = "Depth_label",           facet_col = "Texture")

text: model output : θ̂(pF)=θs−∫0pFsoftplus(s(t))dt\hat{\theta}(\text{pF}) = \theta_s - \int_0^{\text{pF}} \text{softplus}(s(t)) \, dt s(t)s(t) 1-channel Conv1D output. softplus > 0 always, integral strictly increasing, θ̂\hat{\theta} strictly decreasing — monotonicity guaranteed construction, post-processing.

title: soilFlux — Physics-Informed SWRC Modelling
text: Physics-informed CNN1D estimating complete soil water retention curve (SWRC) continuous, monotone function matric potential.

title: soilFlux — Physics-Informed SWRC Modelling
text: Classical pedotransfer functions (PTFs) predict volumetric water content (θ) handful fixed pressure heads — field capacity (pF 2.0), wilting point (pF 4.2), etc. — leaving gaps measurements offering guarantee physical consistency. soilFlux fills entire curve. Given soil sample described texture fractions, organic carbon, bulk density, depth, model returns θ pF value [−2, 7.6], four physics constraints baked loss function strict monotonicity guaranteed architecture.

title: soilFlux — Physics-Informed SWRC Modelling
text: Van Genuchten (1980) equation standard parametric SWRC model: θ(h)=θr+θs−θr[1+(αh)n]1−1/n\theta(h) = \theta_r + \frac{\theta_s - \theta_r}{\left[1 + (\alpha\,h)^n\right]^{1-1/n}} h = matric potential (cm), θ_r = residual water content, θ_s = saturated water content, α = inverse air-entry pressure, n = pore-size distribution index. Fitting VG requires measured data several pressure heads per sample nonlinear optimiser — parametric form can still violate monotonicity near wet end. soilFlux replaces parameter fitting single forward pass trained neural network: θ̂(pF)=θs−∫0pFsoftplus(s(t))dt\hat{\theta}(\text{pF}) = \theta_s - \int_0^{\text{pF}} \text{softplus}\!\bigl(s(t)\bigr)\,dt s(t)s(t) Conv1D output softplus > 0 everywhere, integral strictly increasing θ̂\hat{\theta} strictly decreasing — monotonicity structural property, post-processing step.

title: soilFlux — Physics-Informed SWRC Modelling
text: model takes 3-D input tensor shape [N, K, p+1], K = 64 knot positions uniformly spaced across pF domain p = number soil covariates. stack Conv1D layers produces slope values knot; passed softplus integrated via trapezoidal rule produce monotone SWRC output: θ̂(pF)=θs−∑k=1Ksoftplus(sk)Δtk\hat{\theta}(\text{pF}) = \theta_s - \sum_{k=1}^{K} \text{softplus}(s_k)\,\Delta t_k saturated water content θ_s predicted separately shallow branch network subtracted, curve anchors correctly wet plateau (pF ≈ −2) oven-dry end (pF = 7.6).
code: Soil covariates (p)       │    Broadcast ──► [N, K, p+1]       │    Conv1D × 3       │    softplus  ──► slopes ≥ 0       │    cumsum    ──► monotone integral       │    θ_s  ─────►  θ̂(pF) = θ_s − ∫ slope dt

title: soilFlux — Physics-Informed SWRC Modelling
text: total training loss combines data term four physics residual terms, weighted tunable λ: ℒ=λ1ℒwet+λ2ℒdry⏟data loss+λ3ℒS1+λ4ℒS2+λ5ℒS3+λ6ℒS4⏟physics loss\mathcal{L} = \underbrace{\lambda_1 \mathcal{L}_{\text{wet}} + \lambda_2 \mathcal{L}_{\text{dry}}}_{\text{data loss}} + \underbrace{\lambda_3 \mathcal{L}_{S1} + \lambda_4 \mathcal{L}_{S2} + \lambda_5 \mathcal{L}_{S3} + \lambda_6 \mathcal{L}_{S4}}_{\text{physics loss}}

title: soilFlux — Physics-Informed SWRC Modelling
text: observed data split pF = 4.2 (wilting point) weighted separately balance large dynamic range curve: ℒwet=1Nw∑∈wet(θ̂−θi)2,ℒdry=1Nd∑∈dry(θ̂−θi)2\mathcal{L}_{\text{wet}} = \frac{1}{N_w}\sum_{\\text{wet}} \bigl(\hat\theta_i - \theta_i\bigr)^2, \qquad \mathcal{L}_{\text{dry}} = \frac{1}{N_d}\sum_{\\text{dry}} \bigl(\hat\theta_i - \theta_i\bigr)^2 λ₁ = 1, λ₂ = 10 — dry end -weighted observed values close zero small absolute errors carry proportionally weight.

title: soilFlux — Physics-Informed SWRC Modelling
text: Collocation points {tjt_j} sampled constraint domain training step. Gradients computed via automatic differentiation (TensorFlow GradientTape). S1 enforces curve becomes straight line dry end — key structural difference Van Genuchten (shaded orange region figure ). S2/S3 act hard barriers prevent network predicting negative positive water content physical bounds. S4 penalises slope saturated plateau (shaded blue region). default weights accessed via norouzi_lambdas("norouzi"); smoother dry end uses norouzi_lambdas("smooth") (λ₃ = 10).

title: soilFlux — Physics-Informed SWRC Modelling
code: library(soilFlux)  # 1. Prepare data ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── data("swrc_example") df <- prepare_swrc_data(swrc_example, depth_col = "depth")  ids     <- unique(df$PEDON_ID) set.seed(42) tr_ids  <- sample(ids, floor(0.70 * length(ids))) val_ids <- sample(setdiff(ids, tr_ids), floor(0.15 * length(ids))) te_ids  <- setdiff(ids, c(tr_ids, val_ids))  train_df <- df[df$PEDON_ID %in% tr_ids,  ] val_df   <- df[df$PEDON_ID %in% val_ids, ] test_df  <- df[df$PEDON_ID %in% te_ids,  ]  # 2. Fit model ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── fit <- fit_swrc(   train_df = train_df,   x_inputs = c("clay", "silt", "bd_gcm3", "soc", "Depth_num"),   val_df   = val_df,   epochs   = 80,   lambdas  = norouzi_lambdas("norouzi"),   verbose  = TRUE )  # 3. Evaluate on test set ────────────────────────────────────────────────── evaluate_swrc(fit, test_df) #> # A tibble: 1 × 4 #>      R2   RMSE    MAE   Bias #>   <dbl>  <dbl>  <dbl>  <dbl> #> 1 0.974 0.0241 0.0163 0.0012  # 4. Predict the full continuous curve ───────────────────────────────────── dense <- predict_swrc_dense(fit, newdata = test_df, n_points = 500)  # 5. Plot ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── plot_swrc(dense, obs_points = test_df,           obs_col   = "theta_n",           facet_row = "Depth_label",           facet_col = "Texture")

title: Build physics residual point sets (S1 – S4) — build_residual_sets
text: df_raw Data frame covariate columns (training split). x_inputs Character vector covariate column names. S1 Number S1 points — dry-end linearity (default 1500). S2 Number S2 points — non-negativity pF0 (default 500). S3 Number S3 points — non-positivity pF1 (default 500). S4 Number S4 points — saturated plateau (default 1500). pF_lin_min Lower pF S1 linearity constraint (default 5.0). pF_lin_max Upper pF S1 linearity constraint (default 7.6). pF0_pos pF theta must >= 0 — S2 (default 6.2). pF1_neg pF theta must <= 0 — S3 (default 7.6). pF_sat_min Lower pF S4 plateau constraint (default -2.0). pF_sat_max Upper pF S4 plateau constraint (default -0.3). seed Integer random seed (default 123).

title: Build the CNN1D monotone-integral SWRC model — build_swrc_model

what: Compute the physics-informed residual loss (Norouzi et al. 2025) — compute_physics_loss
title: Compute the physics-informed residual loss (Norouzi et al. 2025) — compute_physics_loss
text: Evaluates four physics constraints current model weights: S1 (L_lin) Second derivative \(|\partial^2\theta/\partial pF^2|\) dry end \(pF \[5, 7.6]\) near zero (linearity). S2 (L_pos) \(\theta(pF = 6.2) \geq 0\) (non-negativity). S3 (L_neg) \(\theta(pF = 7.6) \leq 0\) (non-positivity). S4 (L_sat) First derivative \(|\partial\theta/\partial pF|\) near zero saturated plateau \(pF \[-2, -0.3]\).

title: Compute the physics-informed residual loss (Norouzi et al. 2025) — compute_physics_loss
code: compute_physics_loss(   theta_model,   res_tensors,   lambda3 = 1,   lambda4 = 1000,   lambda5 = 1000,   lambda6 = 1,   pf_left = -2,   pf_right = 7.6,   training = TRUE )

title: Compute the physics-informed residual loss (Norouzi et al. 2025) — compute_physics_loss
text: theta_model Keras model two inputs: Xseq pf_norm. res_tensors named list four sublists (set1 – set4), containing Xseq pf tensors (output residual_to_tensors()). lambda3 Weight S1 linearity loss (default 1.0). lambda4 Weight S2 non-negativity loss (default 1000.0). lambda5 Weight S3 non-positivity loss (default 1000.0). lambda6 Weight S4 saturation loss (default 1.0). pf_left Left boundary normalised pF domain (default -2). pf_right Right boundary (default 7.6). training Logical passed model call (default TRUE).

title: Compute the physics-informed residual loss (Norouzi et al. 2025) — compute_physics_loss
text: named list TensorFlow scalars: L_phys, L_lin, L_pos, L_neg, L_sat.

what: Fit a physics-informed CNN1D SWRC model — fit_swrc
title: Fit a physics-informed CNN1D SWRC model — fit_swrc
text: main user-facing function training.  Given prepared training (optionally) validation data, builds model, creates physics residual sets, runs training loop early stopping, returns fitted object prediction evaluation.

title: Fit a physics-informed CNN1D SWRC model — fit_swrc
code: fit_swrc(   train_df,   x_inputs,   val_df = NULL,   hidden = c(128L, 64L),   dropout = 0.1,   lr = 0.001,   epochs = 80L,   batch_size = 256L,   patience = 5L,   K = 64L,   lambdas = norouzi_lambdas("norouzi"),   S1 = 1500L,   S2 = 500L,   S3 = 500L,   S4 = 1500L,   pF_lin_min = 5,   pF_lin_max = 7.6,   pF0_pos = 6.2,   pF1_neg = 7.6,   pF_sat_min = -2,   pF_sat_max = -0.3,   wet_split_cm = 4.2,   w_wet = 1,   w_dry = 1,   pf_left = -2,   pf_right = 7.6,   seed = 123L,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Fit a physics-informed CNN1D SWRC model — fit_swrc
text: train_df Data frame training (output prepare_swrc_data()). x_inputs Character vector covariate column names. val_df Optional validation data frame (structure train_df). NULL, early stopping skipped. hidden Integer vector length 2: Conv1D filter counts (default c(128L, 64L)). dropout Dropout rate (default 0.10). lr Learning rate Adam optimizer (default 1e-3). epochs Maximum number epochs (default 80). batch_size Mini-batch size (default 256). patience Early-stopping patience multiples 5 epochs (default 5). K Number knot points (default 64L). lambdas Named list loss weights; use norouzi_lambdas() generate (default: norouzi_lambdas("norouzi")). title: CNN1D monotone-integral model architecture — model
text: Build physics-informed 1-D CNN monotone integral output layer, described Norouzi et al. (2025).

title: CNN1D monotone-integral model architecture — model
text: model takes two inputs: Xseq_knots: 3-D tensor shape [N, K, p+1] — observation, p scaled covariates broadcast across K knot positions, knot positions form last channel. pf_norm: 2-D tensor shape [N, 1] — query pF value normalised [0, 1]. output satisfies: $$\hat{\theta}(pF) = \theta_s - \int_0^{pF} \text{softplus}(s(t))\,dt$$ \(s(t)\) 1-channel convolutional output.  Monotone decrease guaranteed construction integrand always positive.

title: CNN1D monotone-integral model architecture — model
text: Norouzi, . M., et al. (2025). Physics-Informed Neural Networks Estimating Continuous Form Soil Water Retention Curve. Journal Hydrology.

code: swrc_example

title: Example soil water retention dataset — swrc_example
text: data frame 4 800 rows 14 columns: PEDON_ID Character. Unique soil profile identifier (e.g. "P0001"). sand_total Numeric. Total sand content (%). silt Numeric. Silt content (%). clay Numeric. Clay content (%). soc Numeric. Soil organic carbon (%). bd Numeric. Bulk density (g/cm3). sand_vf Numeric. fine sand fraction (%). sand_f Numeric. Fine sand fraction (%). sand_m Numeric. Medium sand fraction (%). sand_c Numeric. Coarse sand fraction (%). matric_head Numeric. Matric head (cm H2O, positive). water_content Numeric. Volumetric water content (m3/m3). Approximately 1% values NA simulate missing measurements. depth Character. Depth interval (e.g. "0-5", "5-15", etc.). Texture Character. USDA texture class (one : Sand, Loamy Sand, Sandy Loam, Loam, Silt Loam, Clay Loam, Clay).

title: Example soil water retention dataset — swrc_example
text: Synthetic dataset generated data-raw/create_example_data.R.

title: Example soil water retention dataset — swrc_example
text: dataset contains 120 unique profiles (PEDON_ID) across five depth intervals (0–5, 5–15, 15–30, 30–60, 60–100 cm) eight matric-head points per depth (pF ≈ 0, 1, 1.5, 2, 2.5, 3, 4.2, 7). Profiles evenly distributed across seven USDA texture classes. Water-content observations generated van Genuchten (1980) equation using parameters vary realistically texture depth, small Gaussian noise added.

title: Example soil water retention dataset — swrc_example
text: van Genuchten, M. T. (1980). closed-form equation predicting hydraulic conductivity unsaturated soils. Soil Science Society America Journal, 44(5), 892–898.

title: soilFlux 0.1.0
text: build_swrc_model() — CNN1D monotone-integral architecture (Norouzi et al. 2025). fit_swrc() — Full training loop physics constraints, early stopping, configurable loss weights. predict_swrc() — Point predictions arbitrary pF / matric-head values. predict_swrc_dense() — Dense continuous SWRC curves number soil profiles. predict_theta_s() — Extract modelled saturated water content (θs). evaluate_swrc() — Compute R², RMSE, MAE test set. swrc_metrics() / swrc_metrics_by_group() — Regression performance metrics. norouzi_lambdas() — Loss-weight configurations Norouzi et al. (2025) Table 1. build_residual_sets() / residual_to_tensors() — Physics collocation points (S1–S4). compute_physics_loss() — Evaluate four physics constraints. fit_minmax() / apply_minmax() / invert_minmax() — Column-wise min-max scaling. prepare_swrc_data() — Standardise raw soil data (units, depth parsing, theta normalisation). make_obs_matrices() / make_profile_array() — Build 3-D sequence arrays CNN1D. parse_depth() / parse_depth_column() — Parse depth strings midpoint label. pf_from_head() / head_from_pf() / pf_normalize() / head_normalize() — pF / matric-head conversion helpers. fix_bd_units() / theta_unit_factor() — Automatic unit correction. save_swrc_model() / load_swrc_model() / swrc_model_exists() — Model persistence (weights + metadata). plot_swrc() — Publication-quality SWRC curve figure. plot_pred_obs() — Predicted vs. observed scatter plot metrics. plot_swrc_metrics() — Bar chart comparing model performance. plot_training_history() — Training / validation loss curves. classify_texture() / add_texture() / texture_triangle() — USDA texture classification ternary diagram. swrc_example — Example soil characterization dataset.
